summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for the Moltis messaging platform, porting the concept from an existing TypeScript implementation (openclaw) to Rust using nostr-sdk v0.44, with full parity to existing channels.
summary.outcome: Complete Nostr channel implementation delivered across 3 commits: a new `crates/nostr/` crate (~1700 LOC), web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, E2E tests, documentation, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 decryption support, and an integration test scaffold — all passing cleanly with 23 unit tests and clippy/fmt clean.

repo: ChannelType enum uses #[non_exhaustive] so new variants don't break exhaustive matches in gateway/chat crates that use wildcard arms
channels.offered default list in schema.rs has corresponding test assertions that must be updated when the default changes
Matrix was going through serde `#[flatten]` extra map rather than a named field in ChannelsConfig — unlike telegram/discord/slack/whatsapp/msteams
Contract tests in crates/channels/src/contract.rs are designed for TestPlugin only, not real channel plugins requiring valid credentials
Gateway Cargo.toml uses `dep?/feature` syntax for optional deps in feature lists to avoid pulling in optional crates when the parent feature is enabled alone
moltis_metrics definitions live in crates/metrics/src/definitions.rs with per-channel namespaces; new channels add their own module there
The onboarding flow in onboarding-view.js has both a ChannelTypeSelector (channel picker) and a ChannelStep (form routing) that both need updating for new channels
biome is used for JS linting/formatting; must be run after any JS edits

code.finding: ALL constant and variant count test must be updated together when adding a new ChannelType variant
named_fields() and Default impl for ChannelsConfig must both be updated when adding a named channel field; the all_channel_configs() method iterates both named fields and the serde flatten extra map
Pattern for optional channel deps: add as optional dep, create named feature, add to default features, use dep?/feature syntax in metrics feature to avoid implicit enablement

nostr_sdk::prelude::* imports nip44::{self, *} which brings nip44 functions directly into scope, causing ambiguity with nip04 functions of the same name; use nostr_sdk::nips::nip44::decrypt fully qualified

workflow: nostr-sdk v0.44 has nip04 and nip44 as non-default features that must be explicitly enabled in workspace Cargo.toml
nostr-sdk's nip44::encrypt takes an extra Version parameter vs nip04::encrypt
SecretKey::to_bech32() and PublicKey::to_bech32() return Result<String, Infallible> not String — must call .unwrap() or handle
Client::subscribe() takes a single Filter not Vec<Filter>; Client::disconnect() returns () not Result
client.notifications() creates a temporary that must be bound to a variable before iterating to avoid borrow issues
taplo fmt reorders TOML keys alphabetically — running it globally touches unrelated crates' Cargo.toml files

friction: nostr-sdk nip04/nip44 features not enabled by default — caused initial compilation failures that required iterative discovery
nostr_sdk::prelude::* glob import caused nip44 'unnecessary qualification' lint and name ambiguity with nip04 functions of the same name — resolved by using fully qualified nips module paths
Client::subscribe() API mismatch (single Filter vs Vec<Filter>) and disconnect() return type required checking actual crate source
Metadata::picture() takes a Url type not String — required rewrite of profile.rs
taplo fmt touched unrelated crates' Cargo.toml files (msteams, secret-store, webhooks) as collateral formatting
Promoting matrix to a named ChannelsConfig field was an unnecessary side-effect that had to be reverted in a follow-up commit
OtpState::new() takes a cooldown_secs argument that wasn't initially passed — found only at compile time
open_items: NIP-17 (gift-wrapped DMs) support — more complex event handling, less widely supported currently
NIP-65 relay list advertisement — future enhancement to advertise preferred relays
Real integration tests require NOSTR_TEST_SECRET_KEY and NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY secrets in CI — user needs to generate a dedicated test nsec keypair
Metrics counters/histograms wired but no Prometheus scrape validation or dashboard — same state as other channels at initial ship
NIP-44 outbound defaults to NIP-04 for compatibility; config option to prefer NIP-44 outbound not yet implemented
Seen tracker (seen.rs) does not yet persist to disk — debounced writes to data_dir()/nostr_seen.json were planned but not implemented
